title: fraxinus odontocalyx
intro: Fraxinus odontocalyx, also known as the Mexican ash tree, is a deciduous tree native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus odontocalyx include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and bark, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
